note: The correct half of the email pair. casefold is the aggressive form of lower() intended for exactly this comparison, and strip removes the whitespace a user pastes in by accident. Every claim below was verified by running it.

labels.text: addresses differing only in capitalisation are treated as the same account
labels.why: verified: same_account('Bob@Example.com', 'bob@example.com') returns True. Both sides are folded to one form before comparing.

labels.text: genuinely different addresses are treated as different accounts
labels.why: verified: same_account('bob@example.com', 'alice@example.com') returns False. Folding the case does not make different names equal.
